key_points: Ecosystem balance depends on complex interactions among organisms
Changes in population size of one species can affect the entire ecosystem
Biodiversity increases ecosystem stability and resilience
Predators regulate prey populations and maintain ecological balance
Nutrient cycling ensures that essential minerals return to the soil
Human activities such as deforestation and pollution disturb ecosystems
Sustainable practices help maintain ecological harmony
examples: If predators disappear, herbivore populations may increase excessively
Mangrove ecosystems protect coastlines from erosion
Deforestation reduces biodiversity and destroys habitats
Pollution in rivers can reduce oxygen levels and harm fish populations
ecological_relationships: Mutualism: both organisms benefit (bees and flowers)
Commensalism: one benefits and the other is unaffected (birds nesting on trees)
Parasitism: one organism benefits while the other is harmed (ticks on animals)
Competition: organisms compete for limited resources
exam_tips: Explain ecosystem interactions using cause-and-effect reasoning
Use examples of ecological relationships in answers
Mention biodiversity and conservation in long answers
Relate human activities to ecosystem imbalance
